data.title: Poland's zloty slumps as Trump backs use of force against Russian jets
data.description:
Poland’s zloty slipped to its weakest level this month after U.S. President Donald Trump said NATO members should shoot down Russian aircraft violating allied airspace, raising fears of escalation.

The currency fell 0.2% against the euro on Wednesday, underperforming peers such as the Hungarian forint and Czech koruna. Trump’s remarks, quickly echoed by Polish Foreign Minister Radoslaw Sikorski, followed repeated Russian drone and jet incursions into NATO territory.

Analysts said investors worry a Russian plane could be downed if tensions flare. Prime Minister Donald Tusk has already vowed to defend Polish skies after nearly 20 drones crossed into its airspace in one night.

Despite recent volatility, the zloty remains steady versus the euro this year and is up 14% against the dollar.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Therma Bright and Gen-X Med partner to distribute Venowave™
Starbucks baristas are still fighting to secure a formal union contract
data.description:
Starbucks baristas are more than three years into their battle to work out a finalized union contract with the company.

The baristas represented by the Workers United union have seen their formal talks with Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) progress slowly this year, with some forward motion on non-economic issues, but major disputes still remaining over wages, scheduling, benefits, and understaffing. Mediation has also failed to lead to a resolution.

Earlier this year, Workers United rejected Starbucks' (NASDAQ:SBUX) latest economic offer, which it said failed to make real improvements to pay or workplace conditions.

On September 22, Starbucks (SBUX) workers in Seattle participated in a "practice" picket outside the large Starbucks Roastery, the company's flagship store. The union also highlighted that more than 45 major organizations sent a letter to Starbucks (SBUX) CEO Brian Niccol urging the company to finalize fair union contracts. "We will continue to back union workers’ fight, including by not crossing barista picket lines at Starbucks if they feel striking is necessary," warned the letter.

The first Starbucks (SBUX) store to vote to unionize was located in Buffalo, New York. That watershed vote took place in December 2021.

For its part, Starbucks (SBUX) has steadily maintained it has a deep commitment to a "best-in-retail" workplace, citing competitive compensation and benefits already provided to all partners.

data.title: Thor Industries rallies after topping earnings expectations off operating efficiencies
data.description:
Thor Industries (NYSE:THO) shot higher in early trading on Wednesday after the company topped fiscal fourth-quarter earnings expectations.

Revenue was down 0.4% year-over-year for the RV maker to $2.52 billion, topping the consensus mark by $200 million. The Indiana-based company noted that the North American Towable and North American Motorized segments saw market share inflect in the period as strategic initiatives executed throughout the fiscal year continued to gain traction. 

Sales in the North American Towable RVs segment fell 4.6% to $888.7 million, while sales in the North American Motorized RVs segment rose 7.8% to $557.4 million. The European RVs segment saw a 2.2% decline in revenue to $923K.

Notably, dealer inventory turns improved sequentially, and the channel was said to be appropriately positioned heading into the fall.

Diluted earnings per share for the quarter was $2.36 vs. $1.23 consensus and $1.68 a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA of $209.5 million was reported vs. $218.4 million a year ago.

Looking, the company sees FY26 revenue of $9.0 billion to $9.5 billion (midpoint $9.25 billion) vs. $9.6 billion consensus and EPS of $3.75 to $4.25 (midpoint $4.00) vs. $4.29 consensus. Strategic organizational restructuring progressed during FQ4 is seen as putting Thor Industries (NYSE:THO) in a favorable position to achieve additional operating efficiencies. "As we enter fiscal 2026, our liquidity position provides us significant flexibility to take advantage of potential opportunities, including any stock price dislocations," highlighted CFO Colleen Zuhl.

Shares of Thor Industries (THO) were up 8.3% in premarket trading.

data.title: CTO Realty sets new $10M stock buyback plan
data.description:
• CTO Realty Growth (NYSE:CTO) announced that its board has approved a new $10 million stock repurchase program. The company also completed its previous $5 million program, repurchasing about $4.3 million of stock over the past 60 days.
  • Under the new plan, CTO can buy up to $10 million of its shares through open market transactions, block trades, private deals, or trading plans under Rule 10b5-1, depending on market conditions. The program does not require the company to repurchase a set amount and can be modified, paused, or ended at any time.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Johnson Controls appoints Todd Grabowski to lead Americas segment
data.description:
• Johnson Controls (NYSE:JCI) on Wednesday announced the appointment of Todd Grabowski as vice president and president, Americas, effective Oct. 1, 2025.
  • Grabowski will be responsible for strategy and execution in the Americas, focused on driving organic growth through expanding market share and enhancing customer outcomes.
  • Grabowski, who most recently held the role of president of Global Data Centers & Applied Equipment, will report to CEO Joakim Weidemanis and serve as a member of the company's executive committee. 
  • He succeeds Nate Manning, who will depart Johnson Controls at the end of the calendar year to pursue other opportunities.

data.title: Carter's plays defense with a poison pill to prevent a hostile takeover
data.description:
Carter's (NYSE:CRI) announced on Wednesday that it adopted a temporary stockholder rights plan because one investor unexpectedly bought a large amount of its shares and may be buying more. The plan is aimed at preventing any investor from secretly taking control of the company without properly compensating all shareholders or giving the board time to evaluate deals.

The company will give stockholders a special right attached to each share they own starting October 3. Those rights only become active if someone buys 15% (or 20% for certain investors) of shares without board approval. If triggered, affected shareholders can either swap the right for a share of Carter's (NYSE:CRI) or buy Carter's (NYSE:CRI) at a reduced price, which will make it very costly for anyone trying a hostile takeover.

The buyer of the large stake in Carter's (CRI) is RWWM Inc., which is a California-based registered investment advisor that manages over $1.3 billion in assets.

Shares of Carter's (CRI) were down 0.8% in premarket trading to $29.28 vs. the 52-week range of $23.38 to $68.69. Short interest on Carter's (CRI) stands at 11.6% of the total float.
